meta.purpose: Historically grounded narrative simulation: law-bound story objects, bounded character cognition, world-state-shaped pressure and relationships — not raw LLM improvisation.
meta.mapGeneratedFor: AI / human onboarding after thread context loss
meta.repositoryPathHint: Next.js app + Prisma/Postgres; admin UI under /admin/*

core_law_summary.inference_vs_simulation: External LLM may act as inference substrate; Campti constrains what a character can know, infer, express, and risk by era (world state), body, training, pressure, and relationships.
core_law_summary.personality_constant_expression_filtered: Core personality can be stable; expression and legibility change with governance, norms, masking, disclosure, and rumor fields.
core_law_summary.no_isolated_minds: Behavior is framed as emerging from intelligence + pressure + environment + relationships + continuity — not a single IQ or diagnosis label.

architecture_layers: Ontology & registries (what kinds of objects exist)
Bounded cognition: intelligence, knowledge horizon, maturity, biology (Stage 5.5)
Relationships, desire, masking, disclosure (Stage 6.1 in build sequence)
Continuity: trauma, consequence, rumor, education, health envelopes (Stage 6.5)
Symbols/motifs (parallel Stage 6 in sequence file — see numbering note)
Future: brain assembly (product roadmap), scene runner, timeline, voice, memory, branches, runs

roadmap_numbering_note.issue: The file lib/campti-build-sequence/stages.ts defines CAMPTI_BUILD_STAGES with numeric stage IDs 1–15. Stage 7 there is already 'Timeline / Anchor Engine'. Product discussion also uses 'Stage 7' for Brain Assembly — these names collide.
roadmap_numbering_note.recommendation: Introduce a distinct slug for brain work (e.g. brain-assembly-engine) in stages.ts or a separate ROADMAP.md when implemented; do not overwrite timeline stage metadata without an explicit renumbering decision.

product_next_steps.title: Character Brain Assembly / Cognitive Envelope Engine
product_next_steps.intent: First assembled 'brain wrapper': combine existing profiles into usable per-moment outputs (notice, meaning, regulation, relational safety, decision).
suggested_outputs: Meaning-making envelope (internal explanation + idiom + danger/shame/omen framing)
Relational safety envelope (who is safe, what can be said, disclosure cost)
Decision envelope (options, forbidden options, likely action, cost of speech)
implementation_options: Five tables: CharacterPerceptionEnvelope, CharacterMeaningEnvelope, CharacterRegulationEnvelope, CharacterRelationalSafetyEnvelope, CharacterDecisionEnvelope (each keyed by personId + worldStateId or scene slice as designed)
Or one CharacterBrainState with five JSON sections for envelopes
inputs_to_compose_from_existing_schema: CharacterGovernanceImpact, CharacterSocioEconomicProfile, CharacterDemographicProfile, CharacterFamilyPressureProfile, WorldGovernanceProfile, WorldPressureBundle
CharacterMaskingProfile, CharacterDesireProfile, RelationshipProfile, RelationshipDisclosureProfile, RelationshipDynamicState, WorldRelationshipNormProfile, CharacterRumorReputationProfile
PlaceState, environment nodes, risk — when scene is in scope
product_next_steps.explicitly_not_in_scope_first_pass: Full scene simulation loop; prose generation; spiritual engine; rumor propagation dynamics — assembly is structured outputs + stubs until runner exists.

product_next_steps.title: Scene-time decision runner (product 'Stage 7.5')
product_next_steps.intent: Given a moment (scene + world state), invoke assembly + constraints to produce bounded choice / speech legality.

stages.title: Constitutional Core
stages.goal: No narrative object exists without legal status.

stages.title: Ontology / Registry Spine
stages.goal: Every object inherits from a known ontology.

stages.title: Character Engine
stages.goal: Characters as bounded simulation systems with triggers and choice bandwidth.

stages.title: Place / Environment Engine
stages.goal: Place changes what can be seen, said, remembered, and survived.

stages.title: Pressure / Historical Order Engine
stages.goal: External forces compress choice before characters choose.

stages.title: Intelligence, Knowledge Horizon & Maturity Engine
stages.goal: Historically and biologically bounded cognition — not a single IQ scalar.

stages.title: Relationship, Desire & Masking Engine
stages.goal: Bonds, desire, masking, disclosure under world norms.

stages.title: Trauma, Health, Consequence Memory, Rumor & Education Engine
stages.goal: Continuity over time; era-shaped health and learning — not simulation runs yet.

stages.title: Symbol / Motif Law Engine
stages.note: Numeric id 6 is listed after 6.5 in the array; order is intentional for Cursor execution messaging, not numeric sorting.

stages.title: Timeline / Anchor Engine
stages.goal: Temporal determinism — fixed and elastic moments.
stages.collision_warning: Product roadmap may use 'Stage 7' for Brain Assembly — different concept; see roadmap_numbering_note.

stages.title: Scene Constraint Engine
stages.goal: Scene simulation law — legality before prose.

stages.title: Meta-Scene / Experience Engine
stages.goal: Separate event truth from reader experience.

stages.title: Research-to-Story Binding Engine
stages.goal: Traceable truth flow from archive to story.

engine_and_service_modules.description: Domain logic and stubs live under lib/; server mutations under app/actions/; admin reads often via lib/data-access.ts with safe() fallback when DB unavailable.
notable_stubs: assembleCharacterContinuityEnvelope (lib/continuity-order.ts) returns null — future full merge
relationship-order: evaluateDisclosureSafety, evaluateDesireConstraint, evaluateMaskingPressure, assembleRelationshipCollisionEnvelope, evaluateRelationalTabooRisk
continuity-order: evaluateTraumaActivation, evaluateConsequenceLearning, evaluateRumorRisk, evaluateEducationConstraint, evaluateHealthCrossLayerEffects
pressure-order: assembleCharacterPressureStack and several evaluate* stubs
intelligence-engine: assembleCharacterCognitiveEnvelope implemented as deterministic merge; deeper simulation TBD

data_access_bundles.getCharacterIntelligenceBundle: person × world: intelligence, development, biological, worldKnowledge, worldExpression
data_access_bundles.getCharacterPressureBundle: person × world: governance, socio-economic, demographic, family pressure, characterState
data_access_bundles.getCharacterRelationshipBundle: person × world: masking, desire, network summary, world relationship norms, dyads involving person
data_access_bundles.getCharacterContinuityBundle: person × world: trauma, consequence, rumor, education, learning envelope, world education norm, world health norm, physical/mental/emotional health, health envelope, plus intelligenceRef, pressureRef, relationshipRef

migrations_and_ops.windows_note: If npx prisma generate fails with EPERM renaming query_engine-windows.dll.node, close processes locking node_modules/.prisma/client and retry.
migrations_and_ops.build_safety: data-access uses safe() try/catch patterns so missing DATABASE_URL does not always crash build paths — verify locally.

seeding.note: Continuity seed includes world education norms, health norms, Alexis examples — additive upserts.

current_status_snapshot.implemented_in_schema: Broad coverage: law, ontology, character, world state, pressure, cognition, relationships, continuity, health, environment, narrative objects (scene/chapter/event), fragments, claims.
not_yet_done_product_priority: Brain Assembly Engine (composed envelopes or CharacterBrainState)
Scene-time brain runner consuming assembly + scene constraints
Full simulation evaluation loops (many stubs intentionally)

conventions.world_slice_key: Most character-simulation tables use personId + worldStateId with @@unique where one row per era.
conventions.json_fields: Flexible era language and structured payloads; avoid DSM-first labels in user-facing story fields — health uses simulationLayer vs worldFacingHealthNarrative pattern.
conventions.dyad_normalization: RelationshipProfile uses personAId < personBId lexicographic normalization in app logic.
